question.label: Decide vs escalate
question.name: On decide versus escalate, what cases feel least clear right now?
question.description: Drills into the specific decision situations she is still decoding so the manager can remove ambiguity.

answer: Cross-team conversations move fast and I sometimes leave unsure about who committed to what.

assessment.note: She names a specific clarity gap in fast cross-team conversations: leaving without clear ownership or commitments.

new_queue.label: Committed to what
new_queue.name: What makes it hard to tell who committed to what in those conversations?

new_queue.label: Alignment friction
new_queue.name: What part of the alignment work is taking more effort than it should?

new_queue.label: Manager boundaries
new_queue.name: What boundaries from me would help most right now?

history.answer_excerpt: I am settling in, but I still feel like I am learning the unwritten rules in every meeting.
I can draft plans, though I am not always sure when I should decide versus escalate.
I am settling in, but I still feel like I am learning the unwritten rules in every meeting.
Cross-team conversations move fast and I sometimes leave unsure about who committed to what.
